channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: North Carolina environmental rules are 'insult to injury' for Helene recovery efforts, lawmaker says
item.description: Rep. Jake Johnson, a North Carolina General Assembly member, spoke to Fox News Digital about criticism of the state's environmental agency after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas sues Biden administration for not providing data on noncitizens
item.description: Attorney General Ken Paxton accused the federal government of failing to provide citizenship data for registered voters, even as he acknowledged that the vast majority of the people on the rolls are lawful voters.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris speaks about creating economic opportunity for Latino men; Trump airs familiar grievances
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris on Tuesday said she would work to bring more funds to community banks to help Latino men secure small business loans, while Republican Donald Trump's economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters devolved into a tirade of insults against his op
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, JILL COLVIN and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden calls for Trump to be 'politically' locked up at New Hampshire event
item.description: On Tuesday, President Biden called for former President Trump to be politically "locked up" over accusations that he is a threat to democracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Housing on the ballot: Harris, Trump push different plans for tackling housing affordability crisis
item.description: Millions of Americans can’t afford to buy a home or rent a suitable apartment, making housing a central issue for voters in the upcoming presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sally Ho And Alex Veiga, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: More Republicans are voting early, helping break records. Nearly 19 million ballots cast so far
item.description: With former President Donald Trump’s encouragement, Republicans are voting early again, flocking to the polls for in-person voting ahead of Election Day and helping push the national number to nearly 19 million.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Breaking down the numbers in race for Pennsylvania
item.description: Pennsylvania is worth 19 electoral votes and is pivotal to the successes of the Republican and Democratic Parties come Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in North Carolina on Election Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — North Carolina voters will cast ballots for president and governor in the Nov. 5 general election while many in the state are still dealing with the aftermath and massive destruction caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How third-party candidates could make a difference in swing states
item.description: Third-party candidates will be on the ballot in all of the top battleground states, drawing increased scrutiny over how they could influence the outcome of the presidential election in two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early voting kicks off in battleground Wisconsin with a push from Obama and Walz
item.description: In-person early voting has kicked off across battleground Wisconsin, with former President Barack Obama and Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz hosting a rally in liberal Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Millions of voters have already cast ballot for Nov. 5 election
item.description: Early in-person and mail-in ballots have begun pouring in across the country, and the tally in each state reveals mounting voter enthusiasm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has made more than 100 threats to prosecute or punish perceived enemies
item.description: An NPR investigation has found that since 2022, Trump has repeatedly threatened to investigate, prosecute, jail or otherwise punish his political opponents, rivals and even private citizens.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Trump and Harris' closing message to Republicans are as different as they come
item.description: With two weeks to go until voting closes on Election Day, former President Donald Trump and Vice President Harris made two separate but equally important pitches to Republicans on the campaign trail Monday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: A frustrated Supreme Court to look at one version of judge shopping
item.description: Both liberal and conservative lawyers have judge-shopped, but in recent years, some conservative-leaning groups have been laser focused on bringing their challenges in the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: The race is so close. Here's what happens if there's a 269-269 Electoral College tie
item.description: It’s very unlikely — but theoretically possible — that the presidential election ends with an Electoral College tie. If it did come to pass, here's how the next president would be determined.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez, MPR News

item.title: Some Tampa Bay counties require vote-by-mail postage
item.description: Early voting is underway this week, and supervisors of elections also expect an increase in the return of vote-by-mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC
